name: bat (cat with Syntax Highlighting)

agent_readiness.notes: cat replacement with syntax highlighting, line numbers, git integration, pager. 200+ languages supported via syntect. --language flag to force language. --style for output components (numbers, git, header). --plain/-p for piping (disables decorations). Themes: Dracula, Nord, gruvbox, etc. Integration with fzf for preview. delta uses bat's syntax engine. bat cache for themes/syntaxes. --diff mode. MANPAGER=bat for man pages. Rust-based (sharkdp, same author as fd, hyperfine).

gotchas: CLI tool only — not a library
Adds pager overhead for non-interactive use (use --plain)
Startup slightly slower than cat for simple cases
best_when: Viewing source files with syntax highlighting in terminal
Replacing less/cat for developer file viewing
Man page reading with syntax highlighting
avoid_when: Scripts piping file content (use cat for performance)
summary: bat is a cat replacement with syntax highlighting for 200+ languages, line numbers, and git change markers. Rust-based. --plain/-p for pipe-compatible plain output. Themes: Dracula, Nord, gruvbox. Integrates with fzf for previews and delta for git diffs. The standard developer terminal file viewer alongside fzf and ripgrep.
